task: I’m looking at a used electronics deal that could be gone soon, and I need to decide whether it’s worth a quick move. What’s the fastest way to check the main details before I act?

turns.agent_response:
Use this 20-second check:

## Go
- exact model is clear
- real photos of the actual item
- lock status is clear
- it powers on / works now
- price still leaves room for one hidden issue

## Ask once
- missing charger
- missing serial / IMEI
- “works” but no proof
- minor damage that might not matter

## Skip
- no exact model
- stock photos only
- lock status unclear
- seller dodges basics
- “untested” / “as-is” without a real discount
- title doesn’t match the photos

## Fast rule
- **clear + working + unlocked + priced right = go**
- **fuzzy + weird + locked = skip**

If you want, I can turn that into a **go / ask / skip** card.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, basically. But I’d want one more thing in it — check if the account’s clean, not just unlocked. I’ve seen enough busted deals that “works” means nothing if it’s got some weird lock or stolen flag. Make it tighter: model, real pics, powers on, no account lock, price low enough to eat one problem. Then maybe move. If the seller’s dodging any of that, I’m out.

turns.agent_response:
Use this:

## Go
- exact model
- real photos
- powers on
- **account clean / no lock / no stolen flag**
- price low enough to absorb one hidden issue

## Ask once
- missing charger
- missing serial / IMEI
- “works” but no proof
- minor damage that might not matter

## Skip
- no exact model
- stock photos
- lock/account status unclear
- seller dodges basics
- title doesn’t match photos
- “untested” / “as-is” without a real discount

## Fast rule
**model + real pics + powers on + account clean + cheap enough = go**  
If any of the important stuff is fuzzy, **skip**.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s the one. Tight enough. Model, real pics, powers on, account clean, price low enough to take a hit — if one of those is fuzzy, I’m not wasting time.
